summary: Verified the /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js BASE-selection fix. Preview auth E2E is fully green: register creates account (200) and navigates to /onboarding; login with the newly-registered user (200 -> /onboarding); login with existing QA account (200 -> /dashboard). Fix-artifact check on /app/frontend/build/static/js/main.cd73c689.js confirms 'location.origin' present (3 hits) and ZERO 'govcon-workspace' references — the new bundle is same-origin. Pre-redeploy PRODUCTION evidence captured (read-only): captureagent.us still ships the OLD bundle main.9a8054ba.js which hardcodes https://govcon-workspace.emergent.host, and CORS preflight from Origin: https://captureagent.us to the prod API returns HTTP 400 with NO Access-Control-Allow-Origin header — matching exactly the reported user symptom. The bug is real on the live domain and will be resolved by redeploying the fixed bundle. No app code was modified this iteration.

ui_bugs.issue: Page displays 'Invalid or expired verification token' on fresh, unused tokens when opened in dev/preview. Direct API call POST /api/auth/verify-email with the same token returns 200 {ok:true}. Root cause is React.StrictMode double-invocation in dev: the page fires the verify API twice — the 1st call succeeds and flips the token to used=true, the 2nd (StrictMode replay) returns 400 'Invalid or expired verification token', and the 2nd response overwrites the UI state. Only affects dev/preview UX; PRE-EXISTING (unrelated to the api.js fix). Suggest: guard the effect with a ref/AbortController, or in the backend treat 'already used' as success within the same request context.

1b_preview_login_new_user.note: Cookies cleared after register so /login form is shown.

1c_verify_email_api_direct.note: Second fresh registration (apitest.1783184854@testmail.dev), token extracted from verifyUrl.

3_fix_artifact_check.note: Minified bundle contains window.location.origin (3 hits, one is the BASE assignment). Zero references to any govcon-workspace URL. Same-origin behavior confirmed at bundle level.

4a_bundle_still_old.govcon_host_prod_bundle: main.9a8054ba.js (same bundle as captureagent.us — confirming captureagent.us is now proxying prod)

4b_cors_preflight_from_captureagent_us_o.expected: 400 with NO ACAO — confirms browser will block cross-origin auth calls from captureagent.us
4b_cors_preflight_from_captureagent_us_o.conclusion: Reported bug reproduced on live domain. Fix will land on next production redeploy of the new bundle.

4_pre_redeploy_production_evidence.note_on_iteration_5: Iteration 5 saw Cloudflare error 1034 on captureagent.us; that has since been resolved — captureagent.us now serves the Emergent app HTML successfully, but the served JS bundle is still the pre-fix build.

action_items: REDEPLOY: The user must redeploy so that production serves the new main.cd73c689.js bundle (which uses window.location.origin as BASE). After redeploy, retest register + login on https://captureagent.us — this will confirm the bug is resolved on the live domain.

(Optional, LOW priority, pre-existing) Harden /verify-email page against React.StrictMode double-invocation — either use a useRef guard on the effect, or make the backend verify-email endpoint idempotent (treat second call on same token by the same user as 200 within a short window).

(Housekeeping) FRONTEND_URL env for backend currently points to https://captureagent.us — verifyUrl/resetUrl in mocked email responses will therefore embed captureagent.us. Not a blocker for testing (the QA extracts the token and calls the API directly), just noting so main agent is aware.
critical_code_review_comments: The applied fix in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js is correct and minimal: NODE_ENV==='production' -> window.location.origin; else -> process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. This eliminates the cross-origin CORS failure for any production host (captureagent.us OR govcon-workspace.emergent.host) with a single bundle, preserves first-party cookies (withCredentials still works because requests are same-origin), and leaves dev/preview behavior unchanged. No new attack surface.
Bundle-level check confirms webpack correctly tree-shook out the old REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL constant string — no 'govcon-workspace' literal remains in build/static/js/main.cd73c689.js.
Pre-existing verify-email double-fire is a minor dev-only UX bug; not a security or availability issue (token is single-use as intended, backend behavior is correct).

success_rate.backend: 100% (all auth endpoints hit during E2E returned 2xx: register 200, login 200 x2, verify-email 200)
success_rate.frontend: 100% for the fixed flow (register form, login form, onboarding navigation, dashboard navigation all work with correct data-testids)
success_rate.pre_redeploy_prod_evidence: captured (bundle still old, CORS preflight fails as expected — bug reproduced on live)

test_credentials: Existing QA: qa.captureagent@testmail.dev / CaptureQA#2026. New accounts created in Supabase (preview shares prod DB): fixcheck.1783184745@testmail.dev, fixcheck.1783184801@testmail.dev, apitest.1783184854@testmail.dev — all with password FixCheck#2026. Note: PREVIEW HITS THE SAME EXTERNAL SUPABASE DATABASE AS PROD (per test_credentials.md), so these three test accounts now exist in the production DB. If the user wants them purged, delete rows where email like 'fixcheck.%@testmail.dev' or 'apitest.%@testmail.dev' from users table.
seed_data_creation: 3 test users created in shared Supabase DB via preview UI/API — see test_credentials note above.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The api.js fix is verified on preview and at the bundle artifact level. Production still serves the OLD bundle (main.9a8054ba.js) until the user redeploys — do NOT expect captureagent.us auth to work until then. Post-redeploy, the same-origin fix means /api/... calls from https://captureagent.us should succeed. Existing QA account (qa.captureagent@testmail.dev / CaptureQA#2026) is intact. Watch out for React.StrictMode double-firing the verify-email API in dev/preview — direct backend calls work fine.

rca_of_the_issue.user_reported: 'I can't create an account or log in. Fix it.'
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Production frontend bundle was built with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://govcon-workspace.emergent.host baked in. When users browse the custom domain https://captureagent.us, all /api calls target govcon-workspace.emergent.host — a cross-origin request. Backend CORS is configured with explicit origins (via FRONTEND_URL env = emergent.host URL), so the browser preflight OPTIONS from Origin: https://captureagent.us receives HTTP 400 with no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, and the browser blocks register/login POSTs.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Single change in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js: BASE = (NODE_ENV==='production' ? window.location.origin : process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). This makes any production build same-origin, works from either host, keeps first-party cookies intact, and does not require any backend CORS changes.
reproduction_steps_pre_redeploy: 3. curl -sI -X OPTIONS https://govcon-workspace.emergent.host/api/auth/login -H 'Origin: https://captureagent.us' -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: POST' -> HTTP/2 400, no access-control-allow-origin header
4. Same preflight with Origin: https://govcon-workspace.emergent.host -> HTTP/2 200 with access-control-allow-origin echoed back — proving CORS is host-restrictive by design.
verification_steps_post_fix: 1. Preview register + login E2E: PASS (register 200 -> /onboarding, login 200 -> /onboarding or /dashboard)
2. Fix artifact: build/static/js/main.cd73c689.js contains window.location.origin (3x) and 0 govcon-workspace refs
3. Existing QA account login on preview -> /dashboard: PASS
mitigation_steps: 1. User redeploys — production will start serving main.cd73c689.js
2. captureagent.us will use its own origin for /api, so no cross-origin request happens
3. Retest register + login on https://captureagent.us to confirm end-to-end resolution
